creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Antoine Brodin from comment #3)
...
> The "worst" fallout are:
> lang/gcc : 1256 skipped

Yes, this one is very strange.  I'm currently mailing Gerald about it, but I suspect it is some sort of undefined behavior in gcc.  The gcc5 port does not suffer the problem, and neither does a hand-build gcc from git master.


> multimedia/libvpx : 659 skipped

Not sure how this is a clang problem, the build log ends with:

gmake[2]: *** No rule to make target 'vp8/encoder/vp8_asm_enc_offsets.c.S', needed by 'vp8_asm_enc_offsets.asm'.  Stop.
Makefile:17: recipe for target '.DEFAULT' failed

That would seem to be some sort of build infrastructural problem instead?


> audio/liblo : 14 skipped
> graphics/opencv : 13 skipped

The -Werror warnings causing these build failures are pointing to actual bugs in the ports themselves.  If nobody is able to fix the bugs (which were already there, just not noticed), they could be compiled without -Werror.


> I will do another run when gcc and libvpx are fixed.

Yes please, thanks.  I will update this bug if we reach some sort of conclusion about the gcc48 port.
